etymology.description: 兵(兵) (bīng, "soldier") refers to the military personnel or troops. 馬(馬) (mǎ, "horse") represents the mounts for soldiers, highlighting the cavalry element. 俑(俑) (yǒng, "figurine") specifically denotes burial figurines or statues, often crafted to serve the deceased in the afterlife.
etymology.interpretation: 兵(兵) (bīng, "soldier") conveys the concept of military troops, while 馬(馬) (mǎ, "horse") emphasizes the involvement of cavalry, indicating the military aspect of the figures. 俑(俑) (yǒng, "figurine") suggests these are not real individuals but sculpted figures meant for burial use, enhancing the sacred duty of serving in the afterlife. Together, 兵马俑 signifies the Terracotta Warriors, illustrating an army of lifelike soldier and horse statues buried to protect the emperor in the afterlife.

examples.chinese: 兵马俑(兵馬俑)是中国古代文化的瑰宝(是中國古代文化的瑰寶)。
examples.pinyin: Bīngmǎyǒng shì Zhōngguó gǔdài wénhuà de guībǎo.
examples.english: The Terracotta Warriors are a treasure of ancient Chinese culture.

examples.chinese: 参观兵马俑(參觀兵馬俑)后，他对中国历史更感兴趣了(他對中國歷史更感興趣了)。
examples.pinyin: Cānguān bīngmǎyǒng hòu, tā duì Zhōngguó lìshǐ gèng gǎn xìngqù le.
examples.english: After visiting the Terracotta Warriors, he became more interested in Chinese history.

examples.chinese: 许多人来到西安(許多人來到西安)，就是为了一睹兵马俑的风采(就是為了一睹兵馬俑的風采)。
examples.pinyin: Xǔduō rén láidào Xī'ān, jiùshì wèile yī dǔ bīngmǎyǒng de fēngcǎi.
examples.english: Many people come to Xi'an just to witness the grandeur of the Terracotta Warriors.
